description: Consolidated canonical financial taxonomy for TabIntelligence extraction pipeline

balance_sheet.definition: Amounts owed to suppliers for goods or services received on credit

relationships.error_message: Accounts payable cannot exceed current liabilities

balance_sheet.definition: Amounts owed by customers for goods or services delivered on credit

relationships.error_message: AR >50% of revenue may indicate collection issues

balance_sheet.definition: Expenses incurred but not yet paid
Accrued liabilities (synonym for accrued_expenses)
Cumulative depreciation of property, plant, and equipment
Accumulated other comprehensive income (synonym for aoci)
Additional paid-in capital (synonym for apic)
Estimated uncollectible accounts receivable
Cumulative gains and losses excluded from net income
Accounts payable (abbreviation)
Amount received from stock issuances above par value
Accounts receivable (abbreviation)
Cash on hand and demand deposits, plus highly liquid short-term investments

validation_checks: cash should be part of current_assets

relationships.error_message: Cash cannot exceed current assets

balance_sheet.definition: Cash and cash equivalents (synonym for cash)
Par value of common stock issued
Right to consideration for goods or services transferred to customer before payment is due (IFRS 15)
Obligation to transfer goods or services for consideration already received (IFRS 15)
Debt instruments that may be converted into equity at the holder's option or upon triggering events
Assets expected to be converted to cash within one year

validation_checks: current_assets = sum of all current asset line items

relationships.error_message: Current assets cannot exceed total assets

Portion of long-term debt due within one year
Total debt (synonym for long_term_debt in balance sheet context)
Revenue received in advance for goods or services not yet delivered
Future tax benefits from timing differences and tax loss carryforwards
Future tax obligations from timing differences
Fixed assets (synonym for ppe)
Excess of purchase price over fair value of acquired net assets

Intangible assets (synonym for intangibles)
Non-physical assets such as patents, trademarks, and software
Inventories (synonym for inventory)
Goods held for sale or raw materials for production

Non-current portion of lease obligations recognized under IFRS 16 / ASC 842

Liquid financial instruments that can be quickly converted to cash

Assets not expected to be converted to cash within one year

relationships.error_message: Non-current assets cannot exceed total assets

Other current assets not classified elsewhere
Other current liabilities not classified elsewhere

Par value of preferred stock issued
Expenses paid in advance for future periods
Property, plant, and equipment (synonym for ppe)
Receivables (synonym for accounts_receivable)
Cumulative net income retained in the business
Asset representing lessee's right to use an underlying asset for the lease term (IFRS 16 / ASC 842)
Simple Agreement for Future Equity; convertible instrument common in seed-stage startup funding
Shareholders' equity (synonym for total_equity)
Debt obligations due within one year

relationships.error_message: Short-term debt cannot exceed current liabilities

balance_sheet.definition: Investments with maturity less than one year
Stockholders' equity (synonym for total_equity)
Tax benefit from prior-period losses that can offset future taxable income
Sum of all current and non-current assets

relationships.error_message: Total assets must equal current + non-current assets
Fundamental accounting equation: Assets = Liabilities + Equity

validation_boosters: Equals sum of current + non-current assets

balance_sheet.definition: Total current assets (synonym for current_assets)
Total current liabilities (synonym for current_liabilities)
Residual interest in assets after deducting liabilities

relationships.error_message: Equity must equal assets minus liabilities

balance_sheet.definition: Sum of all current and non-current liabilities
Sum of total liabilities and total equity; must equal total assets (accounting identity)

relationships.error_message: Total L&E should equal total liabilities + total equity
Total L&E must equal total assets (accounting identity)

balance_sheet.definition: Company's own stock repurchased and held (contra-equity)
Unearned revenue (synonym for deferred_revenue)
Value of partially completed goods in production

cash_flow.definition: Cash paid for business acquisitions
Amortization added back to net income (non-cash expense)
Cash received from sale of property, plant, and equipment
Cash balance at start of period
Business acquisitions (synonym for acquisitions)
Cash spent on property, plant, and equipment

validation_checks: capex should be reasonable % of revenue

real_estate.description: Real estate CapEx typically 15-30% of revenue

cash_flow.definition: Capital expenditures (synonym for capex)
Cash from operations (synonym for cfo)
Cash generated or used by financing activities
Cash generated or used by investment activities

relationships.error_message: Cash from investing is typically negative (outflow)

cash_flow.definition: Cash generated or used by core operating activities

relationships.error_message: Net change in cash should equal sum of CFO + CFI + CFF

cash_flow.definition: Change in accounts payable (positive = increase, negative = decrease)
Change in accounts receivable (negative = increase in AR, positive = decrease)
Change in accounts payable (abbreviation)
Change in accounts receivable (abbreviation)
Change in accounts payable from prior period
Change in accounts receivable from prior period
Change in cash (synonym for net_change_cash)
Period-over-period change in contract liabilities, reflecting timing of revenue recognition vs billing
Period-over-period change in deferred revenue balance, a working capital adjustment in operating cash flow
Change in inventory levels from prior period
Net change in current assets minus current liabilities
Change in inventory (negative = increase, positive = decrease)
Net change in current operating assets and liabilities
Cash received from issuing debt
Debt proceeds (synonym for debt_issuance)
Cash used to repay debt principal
Depreciation added back to net income (non-cash expense)
Cash paid as dividends to shareholders
Dividends paid (synonym for dividends)
Cash balance at end of period

relationships.error_message: Ending cash must equal beginning cash + net change
Ending cash on cash flow should equal cash on balance sheet

cash_flow.definition: Cash received from issuing equity
Operating cash flow minus capital expenditures

relationships.error_message: FCF should equal CFO minus capex

high_confidence_signals: Explicitly labeled as 'Free Cash Flow' or 'FCF'
validation_boosters: Consistent FCF conversion with prior periods
confidence_scoring.ambiguity_notes: Some companies use levered vs unlevered FCF; check definition

cash_flow.definition: Financing cash flow (synonym for cff)
Free cash flow (synonym for fcf)
Investing cash flow (synonym for cfi)
Cash spent on purchasing marketable securities or investments
Cash received from selling investments
Total change in cash from all activities

relationships.error_message: Net change in cash should equal sum of operating, investing, and financing cash flows

cash_flow.definition: Net income as starting point for cash flow statement
Operating cash flow (synonym for cfo)
Other cash flows from operating activities
Proceeds from asset sales (synonym for asset_sales)
Cash inflow from issuance of convertible debt instruments
Cash used to repurchase company shares
Cash used to repurchase company's own shares
Non-cash compensation expense from stock options and grants
Non-cash compensation expense from stock options and grants
Stock buyback (synonym for share_repurchase)
Free cash flow before debt payments (to all investors)

debt_schedule.definition: Outstanding bonds or notes payable
Coupon rate (synonym for interest_rate)
Total debt at the end of the period

relationships.error_message: Closing debt balance should equal opening + drawdowns - repayments

debt_schedule.definition: Contractual restrictions on financial metrics
New debt issued during the period
Contractually required principal repayment
Date when debt principal is due for full repayment
Total debt at the beginning of the period
Optional principal repayment beyond mandatory requirements
Total debt payments (principal + interest) in period

relationships.error_message: Debt service should be negative (outflow)

debt_schedule.definition: Debt service coverage ratio (synonym for dscr)

Debt Service Coverage Ratio (generic). For project finance use dscr_project_finance (CFADS / Debt Service); for corporate borrowers use dscr_corporate (CFO / Debt Service). This item captures DSCR when the distinction is unclear from context.

relationships.error_message: DSCR outside expected range (1.0-10.0x); may indicate extraction error

debt_schedule.definition: Gross debt (synonym for total_debt)
Interest expense calculated on average debt balance
Amount of interest paid on debt in period
Annual interest rate charged on debt instrument

validation_checks: interest_rate should align with interest_expense / total_debt

debt_schedule.definition: Required prepayment of debt principal before maturity
Date when debt principal is due
Total debt minus cash and cash equivalents

validation_checks: net_debt can be negative (net cash position)

debt_schedule.definition: Notes payable (synonym for bonds_payable)
Amount of debt principal repaid in period
Outstanding balance on revolving credit facility
Revolving credit (synonym for revolver_balance)
Debt with priority claim on assets in bankruptcy
Debt with lower priority claim than senior debt
Term Loan A tranche balance
Term Loan B tranche balance
Total outstanding debt (short-term + long-term)
Weighted average interest rate across all debt instruments
Interest paid by adding to the principal balance rather than cash payment, increasing the debt over time
Discount at which debt is issued below par value, amortized over the life of the instrument
Term loan facility allowing the borrower to draw funds over a specified period after initial closing
Pre-approved capacity to increase existing debt facilities without renegotiating the credit agreement
Single-tranche debt combining senior and subordinated layers into one facility with a blended interest rate
Fee charged on the undrawn portion of a committed credit facility
Interest rate margin above the reference rate (SOFR/LIBOR) charged on the debt
Minimum reference rate (SOFR/LIBOR) applied regardless of the actual market rate, protecting lenders from very low rates
Premium or restriction on early repayment of debt, protecting lenders from reinvestment risk
Face value or nominal value of the debt instrument at maturity

Corporate Debt Service Coverage Ratio: CFO (Cash Flow from Operations) / Debt Service. Uses the cash flow statement CFO line — appropriate for corporate borrowers. Distinct from project finance DSCR which uses CFADS.

relationships.error_message: Corporate DSCR outside expected range (1.0x-10.0x)

debt_schedule.definition: Maximum Debt/EBITDA ratio permitted under financial covenants.
Minimum coverage ratio (DSCR or ICR) required under financial covenants.
Buffer between actual ratio and covenant threshold (actual - threshold). Positive = passing; negative = breach.

relationships.error_message: Covenant headroom is negative — covenant breach

debt_schedule.definition: DSCR or leverage threshold below which distributions to equity are prohibited. When dscr_project_finance < restricted_payment_condition, no dividends may be paid. Typically 1.25x–1.50x for infrastructure assets.

DSCR level at which cash is trapped in the project and cannot be distributed. Typically set below the restricted_payment_condition. When dscr < cash_trap_trigger, all available cash is swept to debt service reserve.

Percentage of excess cash flow mandatorily applied to debt prepayment. Common in leveraged finance: 50–100% of excess CFO applied to term loan paydown. Reduces outstanding debt faster than scheduled amortization.
Minimum DSCR above which the cash sweep mechanism is active. Cash sweep only applies after this threshold is met, ensuring debt service is covered first.
Actual dollar amount swept from excess cash flow for mandatory debt prepayment in a given period. Equal to excess_cashflow × cash_sweep_pct. Separate from scheduled principal amortization.

Interest margin at the current leverage or coverage level under a ratchet pricing grid. When the borrower deleverages below a threshold, the margin steps down. Expressed in basis points over SOFR/EURIBOR.
Annual fee paid on the undrawn portion of a revolving credit facility or committed debt. Typically 30–50% of the drawn margin. Represents the cost of maintaining available liquidity.
One-time fee paid at debt closing/funding. Amortised over the debt tenor for effective interest rate calculation. Increases the effective cost of debt above the stated margin.

Fee paid to the arranger(s) for structuring and syndicating the debt facility. Distinct from the upfront fee paid to lenders — arrangement fee is the arranger's compensation. Typically 50–200bps of facility size.

Premium payable on early voluntary prepayment of fixed-rate or high-yield debt. Calculated as NPV of remaining coupons at a reference treasury rate plus spread. Compensates lenders for reinvestment risk on early repayment.

Annual amortization of original issue discount (OID) — the difference between the face value and the issuance price of debt. Increases the carrying value of the debt toward par over the term. Adds to interest expense for tax purposes (non-cash).
Weighted average remaining maturity of all debt tranches, weighted by outstanding balance. Metric for refinancing risk concentration: a low WAM signals near-term refinancing pressure.

Large lump-sum principal repayment due at debt maturity — the residual balance not covered by scheduled amortization. Common in project finance and high-yield structures. Refinancing risk if cash or re-financing not available.

Type of principal repayment schedule: 'linear' (equal payments), 'sculpted' (tailored to CFADS), 'ADSCR-based' (payments sized to maintain target DSCR), or 'bullet' (single payment at maturity). Affects DSCR profile over the debt tenor.
Number of days or months after a covenant breach within which the borrower can remedy the breach (e.g. equity injection) before the lender can accelerate the debt. Typically 30–90 days.

Schedule showing covenant thresholds that tighten (step down) over time, typically annually. Common in leveraged finance: max leverage covenants step down from 6.0x to 4.5x over 4 years. Stored as JSON array of {date, threshold} pairs.
Date on which financial covenants are tested. Typically quarterly (March 31, June 30, September 30, December 31). Covenant breaches at test date trigger cure rights or default.

income_statement.definition: EBITDA adjusted for non-recurring or non-cash items such as stock-based compensation and restructuring charges

relationships.error_message: Adjusted EBITDA should approximate EBITDA + SBC + restructuring charges

income_statement.definition: Systematic allocation of intangible asset costs over useful life
Direct costs attributable to production of goods sold

Cost of sales (synonym for COGS)
Income tax expense for the current period based on taxable income
Change in deferred tax liabilities or assets recognized in the income statement
Systematic allocation of tangible asset costs over useful life
Combined depreciation and amortization expense

income_statement.definition: Results from business units that have been sold or shut down
Cash or stock distributions to shareholders
Earnings before tax (synonym for EBT)
Earnings before interest and taxes; operating profitability measure

income_statement.definition: Earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization

income_statement.definition: Net income divided by weighted average shares outstanding
Net income divided by fully diluted shares outstanding
Unusual, infrequent items affecting net income
Profit from sale of assets above book value
General and administrative overhead expenses

Income tax (synonym for tax_expense)

Expenses incurred in normal business operations excluding COGS

income_statement.definition: Expenses from non-operating activities
Income from non-operating activities
Revenue from other minor or miscellaneous sources
Revenue from sale of physical products or goods
Expenses for research and product development activities
Revenue from ongoing contracts or subscriptions expected to repeat each period
One-time costs associated with reorganizing business operations
Total income generated from primary business activities before any deductions

relationships.error_message: Revenue cannot be less than gross profit

medium_confidence_signals: In operating section but not at top
common_errors: Confusing gross revenue with net revenue

ifrs.guidance: IFRS 15 - Revenue from Contracts with Customers

income_statement.definition: Expenses related to sales and marketing activities
Revenue from provision of services
Selling, general, and administrative expenses
Non-cash expense for equity awards (options, RSUs) granted to employees, recognized on the income statement
Revenue from recurring subscription or membership arrangements

cross_validation.ebt: tax_expense should be 15-35% of ebt for most jurisdictions

income_statement.definition: Revenue based on customer usage or consumption of products or services
EBIT adjusted for non-recurring items and management add-backs. Distinct from reported EBIT. Captures management adjustments above the D&A line.
Total add-backs applied to arrive at Adjusted EBITDA from reported EBITDA. Relationship: adjusted_ebitda = ebitda + ebitda_addbacks.

relationships.error_message: adjusted_ebitda should equal ebitda + ebitda_addbacks

income_statement.definition: EBITDA as reported in the financial statements, before any management add-backs or adjustments. Relationship: adjusted_ebitda = reported_ebitda + ebitda_addbacks. Distinct from adjusted_ebitda — do not conflate.

relationships.error_message: Adjusted EBITDA should be >= reported EBITDA (add-backs are positive)

income_statement.definition: Total of all EBITDA adjustments/add-backs. Relationship: adjusted_ebitda = reported_ebitda + total_addbacks. Alias for ebitda_addbacks; use this canonical when the model presents a single add-back total.

relationships.error_message: adjusted_ebitda should equal reported_ebitda + total_addbacks

income_statement.definition: Net income adjusted for non-recurring items, restructuring charges, and other management adjustments. Distinct from reported net_income — used in management presentations and covenant packages.

Revenue adjusted to include full-period contribution of acquisitions, disposals, or other structural changes as if they had been in place for the full period. Used in acquisition finance and leveraged buyout models.

project_finance.display_name: Cash Flow Available for Debt Service
aliases: Cash Flow Available for Debt Service
Operating Cash Flow Before Debt Service
project_finance.definition: Cash flow available for debt service (CFADS): computed as EBITDA less taxes paid less maintenance capex, adjusted for changes in working capital, BEFORE any debt service payments. This is the primary metric for assessing project debt capacity. Do NOT start from Net Income — that is already post-interest and will double-count on leveraged deals.

relationships.error_message: CFADS must be >= CFAE (CFAE = CFADS minus debt service)
CFADS exceeds revenue; possible extraction error

project_finance.definition: Cash flow remaining after debt service, available for distribution to equity holders (CFADS - Debt Service)

relationships.error_message: CFAE should equal CFADS + debt service (debt service is negative)
CFAE should not exceed CFADS

project_finance.definition: Equity capital injected by sponsors during construction/development phase
Total cash flow stream to equity holders including contributions and distributions
Project development costs including legal, permitting, engineering, and site development (NOT R&D)
Debt service payments shaped to match the project's cash flow profile (vs level repayments)
Debt service reserve account balance, maintained to cover upcoming debt service payments
NPV of CFADS over remaining loan life divided by outstanding debt balance

relationships.error_message: LLCR outside expected range (1.0x - 10.0x)

project_finance.definition: NPV of CFADS over remaining project life divided by outstanding debt balance

relationships.error_message: PLCR outside expected range (1.0x - 10.0x)

project_finance.definition: Internal rate of return on project cash flows before tax
Internal rate of return on project cash flows after tax
Internal rate of return on equity investment (contributions vs distributions)

relationships.error_message: Equity IRR outside expected range (0% - 100%)

project_finance.definition: Total capital investment in the project including capex, development costs, and reserves
Total engineering, procurement, and construction costs for the project
Reserve account funded to cover major maintenance and lifecycle replacement costs
DSCR threshold below which equity distributions are restricted and cash is trapped for debt service

Project finance Debt Service Coverage Ratio: CFADS / Debt Service. Uses CFADS (not corporate CFO) as the numerator — this is the covenant-relevant DSCR for infrastructure and project finance deals. Minimum typical covenant 1.10-1.25x (trigger); distribution lock-up typically 1.25-1.50x.

relationships.error_message: PF DSCR outside expected range (1.0x-10.0x)
PF DSCR below distribution lock-up threshold — cash trap may apply

project_finance.definition: Cash held in the Distribution Reserve Account — a sub-account funded by excess cash flows after debt service, to support future equity distributions. Drawdown requires passing the restricted_payment_condition test.

Reserve account funded to compensate for production shortfalls below P90 generation. Primarily used in renewable energy (solar, wind) project finance. Balance is drawn when actual generation falls below minimum contracted output.
Interest earned on cash held in reserve accounts (DSRA, LCRA, DRA, decommissioning). Typically swept into operating cash flows or credited to the reserve account balance.

Reserve account funded over the asset life to cover decommissioning costs at end of concession or lease. Mandatory in mining, oil & gas, offshore wind, and some regulated infrastructure projects. Funded as a periodic cash charge reducing CFADS.

Metrics specifically used in credit analysis and debt fund underwriting
Metrics measuring how efficiently a company uses its assets and manages operations
Metrics measuring the rate of change in key financial measures over time
Metrics measuring a company's level of debt relative to equity, assets, or earnings
Metrics measuring a company's ability to meet short-term obligations
Key performance indicators specific to manufacturing and production operations
General operational and business performance metrics
Financial metrics expressed on a per-share basis
Metrics measuring a company's ability to generate profit relative to revenue, assets, or equity
Key performance indicators specific to real estate and REIT businesses
Key performance indicators specific to retail businesses
Metrics measuring returns generated on capital, assets, or equity
Key performance indicators specific to SaaS and subscription businesses
Metrics used to assess company valuation relative to earnings, book value, or cash flow
Average annualized revenue per customer contract
FFO - recurring capex; measures cash available for dividends
Average monthly or annual revenue per active user/customer
Annualized value of active recurring revenue contracts
Total sales / number of transactions; average purchase amount per customer visit
Total value of new contracts signed in a period, regardless of revenue recognition timing
Net burn / net new ARR; measures capital efficiency
Rate at which a company spends cash reserves, typically measured monthly
Average cost to acquire a new customer (sales & marketing expense / new customers)
Months required to recover customer acquisition cost from gross margin
NOI / property value; measures return on real estate investment
Actual output / maximum potential output; measures efficiency of production capacity
Days to convert inventory and receivables to cash
Annual pre-tax cash flow / total cash invested; measures equity return
Number of months until cash reserves are depleted at the current burn rate
Percentage of customers who cancel subscriptions in a period
Number of customers lost during a period
Transactions / store traffic; percentage of visitors who make a purchase
Total production cost / units produced
Current assets divided by current liabilities; measures short-term liquidity

Total time from start to finish of production process
(Average inventory / COGS) * 365; days to sell inventory
Average days to pay suppliers

Total debt divided by EBITDA; measures leverage

EBITDA as a percentage of revenue

Net income + depreciation + amortization - gains on sales; REIT earnings metric
Units passing quality inspection without rework / total units produced

Ratio of customer lifetime value to acquisition cost; healthy SaaS target is 3:1
Loan amount / property value; measures leverage
Net new ARR / sales & marketing spend; measures sales efficiency
Value of markdowns / total sales; measures promotional intensity
Usable output / raw material input; measures material efficiency
Average monthly cash expenditure net of revenue
Monthly value of recurring revenue from active subscriptions
Net debt divided by EBITDA

metrics.definition: Net income as a percentage of revenue

relationships.error_message: Net margin should equal net income / revenue

metrics.definition: Revenue retention from existing customers including expansions and downgrades; >100% indicates growth from existing base
Current assets minus current liabilities; measures short-term liquidity

relationships.error_message: NWC should equal current assets minus current liabilities

metrics.definition: Number of new customers acquired during a period
Rental income minus operating expenses; excludes financing and depreciation
Occupied units / total units; measures property utilization
Availability × Performance × Quality; measures manufacturing productivity
Operating expenses / gross operating income; measures efficiency
Operating income (EBIT) as a percentage of revenue

Total units manufactured in a period
Liquid assets divided by current liabilities
(New MRR + Expansion MRR) / (Churned MRR + Contraction MRR); measures growth efficiency

Return on assets (synonym for roa)

Value of scrapped materials / total production value; measures quality and waste
Units sold / units received; measures product demand

Moody's metric representing the weighted average credit quality of a CLO portfolio, where higher values indicate lower credit quality
Weighted average time until principal repayment, measured in years, reflecting the expected duration of a debt portfolio
Percentage of debt principal that has defaulted over a given period
Percentage of principal recovered after a default event, typically expressed as cents on the dollar
Percentage of exposure lost when a borrower defaults, equal to 1 minus the recovery rate
Senior debt (first lien) divided by EBITDA, measuring leverage from the most senior tranche only
First lien debt divided by EBITDA, distinguishing from total or net leverage
EBITDA minus capex divided by fixed charges (interest + scheduled principal + lease payments), measuring ability to cover all fixed obligations
Outstanding loan balance divided by the appraised or market value of the underlying collateral or asset
Ratio of collateral par value to outstanding note balance in a CLO, must exceed trigger level to allow distributions
Ratio of interest income from collateral to interest due on CLO liabilities, must exceed trigger level
Net operating income divided by the total loan amount, used in real estate and asset-based lending as an alternative to LTV and DSCR
